chief_complaint: A 38-year-old male smoker presented with central chest pain radiating to the left shoulder.
history: The patient has acute coronary syndrome with significant 3-vessel disease and underwent urgent CABG using bilateral internal mammary arteries as a Y-graft to the left anterior descending artery and the first diagonal branch. Additional grafts to the posterior descending artery and first obtuse marginal branch were performed using saphenous vein segments.
exam: No specific physical examination findings are mentioned.
labs: ST-segment depression on electrocardiogram, elevated troponin, and coronary angiography revealed significant 3-vessel disease.
